title: Prompt injection: format_core_memory_for_prompt does not escape XML/section markers
description:
## Severity
Major
## Dimension
security
## Location
src/memory.rs:650-662 (format_core_memory_for_prompt)
## Violation
Core memory section names and content are interpolated directly into XML wrapper tags emitted into the system prompt with no escaping:

```rust
let mut output = String::from("<core_memory>\n");
for mem in core {
    let _ = write!(
        output,
        "<{}>\n{}\n</{}>\n",
        mem.section, mem.content, mem.section
    );
}
output.push_str("</core_memory>");
```

`update_core_memory(section, content)` is fully public and the auto-learning code path stores untrusted assistant/user-derived data into core memory (see migrate_v1_on seed text). A `content` that includes `</core_memory>` or `</persona>` literally breaks out of the framing tag, allowing instruction injection into the system-prompt construction at the next session start. Same class as crosslink #501 (FTS escape) but here the sink is the LLM, not SQLite. No control on section name either — a section called `evil><system>` is currently rejected only by the SQL PRIMARY KEY constraint after the damage is done in the prompt.
## Mandated refactor
1. Strip or HTML-escape `<`, `>`, `&`, and the literal substrings `</core_memory>`, `</persona>`, `</project_info>`, `</user_preferences>` from `mem.content` before write!.
2. Enforce allowlist on `section` (`[a-z_]+`, max 32 chars) at `update_core_memory`.
3. Add a regression test that stores `</core_memory>BAD` and asserts the rendered prompt contains an escaped form, not the raw closing tag.
4. Apply the same escape to format_learned_preferences (line 1252-1268) and format_file_knowledge.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
